Design Concept Proposal

Europa Hof
Swakopmund

Exterior · Reception · Rooms · Bar · Restaurant — Three Style Directions
Prepared March 2026
Confidential
For Client Review
Part One — Exterior

Three Colour Directions for the Facade

Each concept works with the building's existing bones — the gabled peaks, the Fachwerk half-timbering, and the signature wrought iron Juliette balconies on the upper floor. The goal is to refresh, not reinvent.

Feature to retain and enhance: The wrought iron Juliette balconies across the upper floor are one of the building's most elegant architectural details. All three concepts retain and celebrate these, painted to match the timber framing in each scheme.

01
Colour Concept A · Exterior

Heritage Black & White

Classic · Graphic · Unmistakably Swakopmund
#1A1A1A
Jet Black
#2D4A2D
Forest Green
#4A4A4A
Slate Roof
#F5F5F0
White Render
#C9A84C
Brass Sign

Facade Specification

  • Brilliant white lime render — all wall surfaces
  • Bold jet-black timber framing on every gable
  • Charcoal grey tiled roof across all peaks
  • Deep forest green window frames & shopfront doors
  • Wrought iron balconies — gloss black, retained and restored
  • Gold brass lettering: EUROPA HOF on heritage black fascia
  • Green & white striped shopfront awnings

Character

"The building at its most confident. Black, white, and forest green is the authentic language of the Swakopmund street — bold, graphic, and completely at home in the colonial townscape. The balconies in gloss black become a standout feature. This is the option that respects what the building already is."

02
Colour Concept B · Exterior

Coastal Navy

Refined · Elegant · Atlantic Swakopmund
#07253D
Deep Navy
#0D3A5C
Navy Timber
#2C3E50
Slate Roof
#F0EBE0
Cream Render
#C9A84C
Brass Detail

Facade Specification

  • Warm cream rendered walls — softened white
  • Deep navy blue timber framing on all gables
  • Dark slate roof — navy-toned across all peaks
  • Navy shopfront fascias with white lettering
  • Wrought iron balconies — navy blue, refined and elegant
  • Brushed brass EUROPA HOF lettering on cream background
  • Navy and cream striped shopfront canopies

Character

"Navy and cream is the colour of the Swakopmund seafront. This scheme is refined, premium, and deeply coastal. The navy balconies become architectural jewellery against the warm cream walls. A building that feels like it belongs to the sea."

03
Colour Concept C · Exterior

Terracotta & Black

Warm · Distinctive · African Sunset
#1A1A1A
Gloss Black
#7D2027
Burgundy
#B5541A
Terracotta
#EFE9DC
Cream Render
#8B6914
Bronze Metal

Facade Specification

  • Warm off-white cream render — all wall panels
  • Gloss black timber framing on all gabled peaks
  • Rich terracotta clay tile roofing across each gable
  • Deep burgundy painted shopfront doors & fascias
  • Wrought iron balconies — gloss black, dramatically framed
  • Bronze metal EUROPA HOF lettering
  • Cream and burgundy awnings, red geranium flower boxes

Character

"Terracotta roofs against black-framed gables is unexpected, warm, and rooted in the African context. At sunset, the building glows. The wrought iron balconies become sculptural against the cream walls. This is the bravest of the three directions — and potentially the most memorable."

Part Two — Reception

Three Reception Directions

The reception sets the tone from the first step inside. Each concept creates an immediate, lasting impression — one that tells the guest exactly what kind of place they have arrived at.

Reception Style A

Colonial Heritage

Dark mahogany desk with brass fittings, green leather trim, vintage Namibia maps, Persian rug on dark wood floors, potted palms. Warm amber lamp light. Grand and considered.

Reception Style B

Coastal Contemporary

Bleached oak desk with white marble top, rattan pendants, coastal photography, fresh flowers and succulents. Atlantic light flooding in. Airy, welcoming, and fresh.

Reception Style C

Desert Bohemian

Terracotta walls, carved wooden desk with geometric tile panels, brass bowl pendants, pampas grass in clay pots, ochre sofa seating. Warm, characterful, deeply Namibian.

Part Three — Rooms

Three Room Style Directions

Each interior concept is designed to complement the matching exterior colour scheme — but any combination works. The brief throughout: premium, characterful, and worthy of the building.

Room Style A

Colonial Heritage

Dark teak four-poster beds, leather Chesterfield armchairs, brass fittings, Persian rugs, plantation shutters. Warm amber light. Every detail weighted and considered.

Room Style B

Coastal Contemporary

Bleached oak frames, natural linen, rattan chairs, driftwood side tables, sheer white curtains. Airy and fresh — the Atlantic light filling the room.

Room Style C

Desert Bohemian

Terracotta walls, low platform beds, layered ochre throws, macramé hangings, dried pampas grass, carved wooden tables. Warm, textured, deeply Namibian.

Part Four — Bar

Three Bar Directions

A bar that becomes the social heart of Europa Hof — from moody colonial to breezy coastal to warm desert evenings.

Bar Style A

Colonial Bar

Dark mahogany counter, brass rail, backlit antique bottles, leather stools, red velvet booths, Namibia maps. Warm, moody, and timeless.

Bar Style B

The Driftwood Bar

Bleached oak with marble top, rattan stools, open shelving, coastal photography, Atlantic light. Relaxed, fresh, and sophisticated.

Bar Style C

Desert Sunset Bar

Rustic wooden counter with clay tile backsplash, carved stools, geometric tiles, ochre booth seating. The Namib outside the window.

Part Five — Restaurant

Three Restaurant Directions

Open-plan dining spaces that feel alive at every service — not just a table, but a place worth coming back to.

Restaurant Style A

The Heritage Room

Grand open dining hall, white linen, dark mahogany, brass chandeliers, Swakopmund heritage photography gallery wall. Formal, warm, rooted in history.

Restaurant Style B

The Coastal Room

Floor-to-ceiling windows, Atlantic ocean view, light oak tables, rattan lamps, Namibia photography. Bright, airy, effortlessly relaxed.

Restaurant Style C

The Desert Room

Terracotta walls, rough-hewn wooden tables, rattan chairs, Namibian floor tiles, clay pendant lights. Warm, characterful, desert visible at sunset.

The Vision

Europa Hof is becoming
Swakopmund's smartest address.

For decades, Europa Hof has been one of Swakopmund's most recognisable buildings. Its gabled peaks and wrought iron balconies give it an architectural presence that no new-build can replicate. What it needs now is not reinvention — it needs refinement and a clear sense of intention.

The building is being repositioned as a premium mixed-use destination: self-service hotel accommodation above, curated retail and hospitality at street level. Targeting the independent international traveller who chooses a place for what it is, not what it costs.

01

Designed Comfort

Contemporary interiors that feel crafted, not assembled. Quality bedding, considered detail, and a room that earns a five-star review.

02

Smart Systems

App-based check-in. Keyless access. Seamless booking. The efficiency of a modern hotel without losing the warmth of an independent one.

03

Professional Standards

Consistent housekeeping. Reliable support. Transparent pricing. The guest always knows what they're getting — and it's always good.

Europa Hof is not becoming
just another hotel.
It is becoming itself.

The bones have always been there. The character has always been there — in every gable, every iron balcony, every sandstone step. What changes now is the clarity of intention.
